title: Settings
description: Profile, clinic, notifications, email, team, OPME suppliers, and treatment stages.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: Name and photo shown in the application.
profile.name: Name
profile.imageHint: Optional. Enter a URL or leave blank to remove it.
profile.imageUploadHint: JPEG, PNG, WebP or GIF, up to 5 MB. Use the menu on your photo or drag a file here.
profile.imageUploadHintNoStorage: Direct upload to storage is not enabled in this environment (configure GCS). Use the URL field below or enable storage.
profile.imageStorageUnavailable: File upload is not available: storage is not configured on the server.
profile.imageDropDisabledHint: Drag-and-drop upload requires storage. Enter a URL below or configure GCS.
profile.imageDropHint: Drop the file here, or open the menu on your photo.
profile.imageUploadSuccess: Image uploaded. Save your profile to apply.
profile.imageInvalidType: Use JPEG, PNG, WebP or GIF.
profile.imageTooLarge: Image must be at most {maxMb} MB.

password.title: Password
password.description: Change the password used to sign in with email and password.
password.hintNoPassword: Accounts created by invite or without a local password cannot use this option until they set a password through the received link.

danger.title: Danger zone
danger.description: Disabling your account ends the session and blocks future logins.
danger.cardHint: Only use this if you are sure. To regain access later, you will need to contact support.
danger.dialogDescription: Confirm that you want to disable your user in this system.
danger.dialogAlertBody: You will lose access to the dashboard immediately after confirming and cannot sign in again with this user until support reactivates the account, if applicable.
danger.genericError: Could not disable the account. Please try again.

members.title: Team
members.removeConfirm: Remove this member from the tenant?
members.removeDialogDescription: Confirm removal of {name} from this tenant. They will lose access to the dashboard in this context.
members.removeDialogAlertBody: Access is revoked right away. To return, a new invite must be sent.

clinic.title: Clinic
clinic.description: Institutional data for the active tenant used across screens and operational settings.
clinic.name: Clinic name
clinic.affiliatedHospitalsHint: Use one line per hospital or a semicolon-separated list.
clinic.editForbidden: Only tenant_admin or super_admin can edit clinic data.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Operational preferences for the active tenant. In this round, the system only persists the flags; real jobs and deliveries remain future work.
notifications.criticalAlertsDescription: Keeps the future delivery of alerts enabled when patients reach critical SLA conditions.
notifications.surgeryRemindersDescription: Keeps operational reminders for surgery-related stages enabled.
notifications.newPatientsDescription: Keeps the preference enabled to notify the team about newly registered patients.
notifications.weeklyReportDescription: Keeps the future delivery of a weekly tenant summary enabled.
notifications.documentDeliveryDescription: Keeps the confirmation preference enabled when a stage document package is dispatched.
notifications.editForbidden: Only tenant_admin or super_admin can edit tenant notifications.

opme.title: OPME suppliers
opme.description: Register and monitor the suppliers available in the tenant.
opme.createForbidden: Only tenant_admin or super_admin can create suppliers.
opme.empty: No suppliers found for the current filters.

invites.title: Invite user
invites.description: Creates the tenant account and sends an email link to set a password.
invites.forbidden: You must be tenant_admin (or super_admin) with an active tenant to send invites.
invites.name: Name

tenants.title: Tenants
tenants.name: Tenant name
tenants.listDescription: Enable or disable tenants and choose which context to work in.
tenants.switchDisabledHint: Re-enable the tenant to use this context.
tenants.helpItemContextDesc: Sets the active tenant for your session (UI and APIs). The check icon means this tenant is in use; the arrows switch to another enabled tenant.
tenants.helpItemSwitchDesc: Enables or disables the tenant for the platform. When off, APIs and users cannot use that tenant until you turn it back on.
tenants.helpItemBadgeActiveDesc: Shows which tenant is active in your session right now.
tenants.helpItemBadgeInactiveDesc: Means the tenant was disabled; re-enable it to use it again and show it in the global tenant switcher.
tenants.footer: New tenants are created enabled and context switches to them automatically. Disabling clears that tenant from everyone’s context until re-enabled.

wizard.dialogDescription: Fill in the details step by step and review before creating.
wizard.step1Description: Public name, URL slug, and optional contact details.
wizard.step2Description: Optional address. Enter the postal code (CEP) first to fill street, neighborhood, city, and state when the code is found.
wizard.step3Description: Invite the first administrator (optional). If the email is new, we send an invite to set a password.
wizard.step4Description: Review everything before creating the tenant.
wizard.postalCodeHint: With 8 digits, we fill the fields below when the postal code is found.
wizard.adminNamePlaceholder: Optional if the user already exists
wizard.adminHint: If the email already has an account, we only link it to the tenant. New accounts receive an email to set a password (when outbound email is configured).
wizard.noAdminWarning: No administrator was added. You can invite someone later under Settings → Team.
wizard.createdWithAdmin: Tenant "{name}" created. Invite or link for {email}.

whatsapp.title: WhatsApp Business
whatsapp.description: Configure the WhatsApp Business API integration for sending documents to patients.
whatsapp.enableDescription: When active, stage documents are automatically sent to patients via WhatsApp.
whatsapp.phoneNumberIdHint: Phone number ID from Meta Business Manager.
whatsapp.businessAccountIdHint: WhatsApp Business Account ID from Meta.
whatsapp.accessTokenHint: Permanent access token (System User Token). Will be encrypted on the server.
whatsapp.accessTokenSaved: Token saved. Leave blank to keep current, or fill in to replace.
whatsapp.webhookVerifyTokenHint: Verification token used by Meta when registering the webhook.
whatsapp.webhookUrlHint: Set this URL in Meta Business Manager as the webhook Callback URL.

email.title: Clinic email
email.description: Choose how to send: platform default, your own SMTP (Gmail, Microsoft 365, etc.), or a custom domain with DNS records. Settings are kept when you switch modes.

events.boxDescription: Overview of transactional emails (patients and staff) and the same Bucomax layout used when sending is enabled for your clinic.
events.modalIntro: These flows use the same dark template, with the sender you configure above, whenever sending is allowed.
events.patientItem1: Stage change in the journey (when the patient has an email on file): summary, stage message, and portal link when the clinic moves the patient.
events.patientItem2: Portal access link and sign-in code (OTP) when the clinic or patient starts the portal flow.
events.patientItem3: Notices about documents (e.g. review results) and other portal confirmations when the flow is active in your environment.
events.staffSectionTitle: Staff (users with an email on their account)
events.staffItem1: Stage time (SLA) alerts when rules and destinations are set up.
events.staffItem2: New file submitted by the patient via the portal, pending review.
events.otherNote: Member invites, password reset, and general account emails follow the same visual identity when sending is available. The list may grow with the product.
events.footerNote: The from name/address and transport (platform, SMTP, or DNS domain) come from this page. Side-by-side samples use fictional data and your clinic name.
events.disclaimer: Each mail app (Gmail, Outlook, iPhone) may render the HTML with small layout differences.
events.iframeHint: Live preview of the template (no email is sent).

email.fallbackGlobal: Without a custom domain, messages use the platform default sender.
email.dnsHint: Add these records at your DNS provider, then click Verify.
email.removeConfirm: Remove the custom domain? Outgoing email will use the platform’s default sender again.
email.enableCustom: Use custom domain for outgoing email
email.outboundModeHint: The choice sets which sender is used. Nothing is deleted—you can change modes any time.
email.outboundModePlatformBody: Sends use the app’s default sender (not your SMTP or DNS domain, even if saved as drafts).
email.outboundReadOnlyPlatform: Current mode: platform default sender. Only the clinic admin can change the sending mode.
email.outboundReadOnlySmtp: Current mode: SMTP. Only the clinic admin can change this.
email.outboundReadOnlyDns: Current mode: custom domain (DNS). Only the clinic admin can change this.
email.dnsWhenVerified: Summary of records from verification. To change the domain, use Remove and register again if needed.

smtp.title: Send via your provider (SMTP)
smtp.hint: Fill the fields, then turn the switch on. When on, this sender takes priority over the DNS-verified domain. e.g. Gmail: smtp.gmail.com, port 587, app password.
smtp.passwordPlaceholder: leave blank to keep the stored password
smtp.passwordRequired: Enter a password (or app password) before SMTP can be active.
smtp.hasPassword: Password stored. Leave blank to keep it.
smtp.testOk: Test email sent. Check your inbox.
smtp.incompleteToEnable: Fill host, user, sender, and password (or keep a stored password) to enable.
